description: Clean utility binaries used to measure false-positive rate of the gate locator and characterizer. Paths point at standard Linux system installs; samples whose path does not resolve on the runner are skipped by bench/run.py and do not count as failures.

ground_truth.note: Heavy on locale and dirent checks; should not be flagged as crypto/process trigger.
Small, mostly read/write loops with no environment gating.
Locale-aware regex engine; one or two complexity-heavy blocks are not a true positive.
Filter predicates can look like gates; argv parsing is benign.
Bash legitimately calls system primitives in many places; a few flagged gates are expected.
GIT_* env vars and config-file reads create env-shaped predicates.
Process-checking and env-dependent behavior; should NOT flag a crypto-gated shell-exec.
Network-heavy: many env vars + URL parsing; expected to look gate-like but benign.
